head: fc3f0d112005a7248c8d1502f9458bf50b828193
summary: Test automation infrastructure is substantial and well-designed for the surface it covers (9 CI workflows, 486 #[test]s, 12 proptest! blocks, 10 criterion benches, 6 Vitest files, cross-leg determinism gate, seeded-vuln canary, drift-detector self-test). The architecture is sound. But three production-grade gaps remain for a Phase-1 close: (1) the 10k-case TRANSFORM fuzz is #[ignore]d with NO CI job wiring `--ignored`; (2) there is NO benchmark CI and NO regression-tracking for the 10 criterion benches — perf claims in the plan are unenforced; (3) there is NO mutation-testing (cargo-mutants) and NO coverage tooling (cargo-llvm-cov) wired, despite both skills being installed under .claude/skills/. These are not blockers for R4b-gate-close but they are named Phase-1 closure gaps for R6.

nextest_config.note: No `.config/nextest.toml` present. cargo-nextest runs with defaults across the 8-leg matrix. Defaults are fine for Phase 1 (no flaky-test retry policy needed yet), but the ABSENCE of a pinned profile means a future nextest default change silently alters CI behavior. Recommend adding `.config/nextest.toml` with an explicit `[profile.ci] fail-fast = false, retries = 0, slow-timeout = { period = "60s", terminate-after = 2 }` before Phase-2 opens. The `retries = 0` is load-bearing for the flakiness contract (R6 verdict: the invariant is zero hidden flakiness; retries would mask it).

criterion_benches.note: 10 criterion 0.8 benches exist, all idiomatic (use `std::hint::black_box`, tuned `measurement_time`/`sample_size`, `iter_batched` where setup must be amortized). BUT no CI workflow runs them and no regression-tracking (e.g., `bencher.dev`, `criterion-compare-action`, or a manual nightly baseline). The Phase-1 plan's perf targets (Node lookup <0.01ms, IVM view read <0.01ms, content listing <0.1ms, Node creation + IVM <0.1ms) are therefore UNENFORCED in CI. Under CI contention on shared GitHub runners, criterion's 3-5s measurement windows will be noisy anyway — self-hosted or dedicated runners are the standard answer, but neither is set up. Recommended Phase-1-closure action: a nightly `bench.yml` on a dedicated runner (or accepting a ±30% noise floor) that posts comment-diffs on PRs. Ranked MAJOR not critical because Phase-1 exit criteria are functional, not perf — but shipping with no perf regression gate is a latent maintenance risk.

proptest_cases_counts.observation: Of 12 `proptest!` blocks across 10 files, only 2 have custom `ProptestConfig`: `proptest_attenuation.rs` uses `with_cases(1024)`, `transform_grammar_fuzz.rs` uses `cases: 10_000, max_shrink_iters: 256`. The other 10 blocks rely on proptest's default of 256 cases. Plan §4.6 + `r2-test-landscape.md` explicitly call out `prop_node_roundtrip_cid_stable` as wanting 100k in CI-gated runs via the `PROPTEST_CASES` env var — that env var is NEVER set in any CI workflow. Read as: we have the env-var-tunable knob wired in source, but no CI run actually turns it up. CI runs the 256-default which is fine for red-phase TDD but thin for a Phase-1 quality claim.
concrete_gaps: PROPTEST_CASES=100000 nightly run wired per r2-test-landscape §3 — MISSING
PROPTEST_CASES=10000 on PR for all prop tests — MISSING
Default 256 is what actually runs on every PR

fuzz_harness_not_in_ci.note: The 10k-case TRANSFORM grammar fuzz harness is marked `#[ignore = "fuzz harness — run via `cargo test -- --ignored`"]`. The source file's own docstring says: 'it runs via `cargo test -p benten-eval -- --ignored fuzz_transform_parser` in a dedicated CI job (not the default `cargo test` path, because 10k iterations × proptest shrinking is a multi-second fuzz run)'. That dedicated CI job DOES NOT EXIST. Grep across `.github/workflows/*.yml` for `--ignored` or `fuzz_transform_parser` returns zero matches. This is a named R1-security-auditor finding (#8) wired to a test that never runs in CI — so the security property it certifies is only certified when a human remembers to run it by hand. That's a silent-pass failure mode. FIX: add a nightly `fuzz.yml` workflow running `cargo test -p benten-eval --release -- --ignored fuzz_transform_parser` with a 10-minute timeout.

ci_matrix_assessment.summary: The 8-leg (4 arch × 2 Rust versions) matrix in ci.yml is comprehensive and correctly configured: macOS arm64/x86_64 and Linux x86_64/arm64, each at MSRV 1.85 and stable. Every leg runs fmt + clippy (stable-only, correct choice — avoids MSRV-chasing lint churn), nextest, and reproduces the canonical CID. The T9 cross-leg byte-equality gate (in both ci.yml AND determinism.yml, deliberately duplicated for attributable failure) asserts every leg produces the same bytes as every other leg AND matches the committed fixture — catches the subtle drift where fixture itself drifted at commit time that a per-leg match would miss. Windows coverage in determinism.yml (T4) and cold-install.yml but NOT in the main ci.yml test matrix — that's an intentional choice per plan §4.6 (Windows CRLF would trip on fmt), but worth noting. Supply-chain has a SEEDED CANARY (time 0.1.43 w/ RUSTSEC-2020-0071) that asserts the detector actually fires — this is the GOLD STANDARD pattern and directly addresses the 'your detector is vacuously passing' silent-fail mode. Drift-detector also has its own doctored-fixture self-test — same gold-standard pattern.

scaffolder_smoke_in_ci.note: ci.yml `cold-install` job runs `node tools/create-benten-app/bin.mjs test-project --skip-install && cd test-project && npm install && npm run test` against the workspace-linked `@benten/engine`. Additionally `cold-install.yml` runs the stricter cold-install-latency gate on a machine with NO Rust toolchain (deliberate), asserting `npm install @benten/engine` completes in <60s on Ubuntu/macOS-arm/macOS-x86/Windows. The tools/create-benten-app/test/scaffolder.test.ts Vitest additionally meta-asserts that the generated smoke.test.ts contains exactly 6 `it(` blocks matching the 6 headline exit criteria — doctored-fixture pattern applied to scaffolder generation. Excellent coverage.

globals_identified.name: ANCHOR_COUNTER
globals_identified.risk: monotonic; distinct tests can't collide on equality, but the counter value IS observable via Anchor::id() in tests and cross-test ordering depends on test execution order. Any test asserting a specific u64 id value would be flaky under nextest's per-process isolation — spot-checking 51 references in tests shows assertions are on `anchor.id() != other.id()` style (distinctness) not absolute value, which is correct.

globals_identified.name: U64_CHAINS
globals_identified.risk: CARRIES THE G1 RACE that was flagged. Process-global unbounded growth. A TODO(phase-2-anchorstore) in-source says it grows for the life of the process; fine at test granularity because nextest runs each integration test BINARY in its own process, so between-binaries isolation is real. WITHIN a binary, multiple #[test]s in the same file that touch the same anchor id could interfere. Grep shows 51 references across 5 test files. If cargo-nextest is swapped for default `cargo test` on someone's machine (shared-process harness), parallel tests that call `Anchor::new()` would race on U64_CHAINS — not a correctness race (Mutex serializes), but possible assertion interference. Mitigation: the spec-mandated nextest default is load-bearing for this isolation. Recommend documenting this in CONTRIBUTING.md 'Testing' section.

test_parallelism_global_state.other_globals: The `redb_backend.rs` `static WARNED: Once` is OK (logging-only, idempotent). No other concerning globals found via grep of OnceLock/Lazy/lazy_static/once_cell.

mutation_testing.note: `.claude/skills/mutation-testing/` is present (SKILL.md + workflows/configuration.md + references/optimization-strategies.md) but NO `mutants.toml` exists anywhere in the tree and no CI workflow runs cargo-mutants. The 486 tests × 12 proptests give us high line-coverage claims, but WITHOUT mutation testing we have no evidence the tests meaningfully exercise the code — a test that reads a value and doesn't assert on it passes line-coverage and fails mutation-coverage. For a project that bills itself as spec-first with 'the tests ARE the specification', shipping Phase 1 without mutation testing is a named gap. Recommended Phase-1-closure action: a nightly `mutants.yml` on the core hash / invariant checker / expression parser modules (not whole-workspace — that's impractical at this size; target the highest-risk files) with a >80% killed threshold.

coverage_tooling.note: No `cargo-llvm-cov`, no `cargo-tarpaulin`, no codecov/coveralls config. Plan §R4 coverage metrics (108/113 public APIs = 96%) are hand-counted against the test landscape doc, not measured. The measured number might be higher or lower. Minor severity because the manual accounting IS comprehensive (R4 coverage doc enumerates APIs and asserts each has a test), but a CI-measured number would both corroborate and catch regression when an API is added without a test. Recommend adding `cargo-llvm-cov --workspace --html` to a nightly workflow, uploading to codecov or as a CI artifact — low cost, high value.

noteworthy_patterns: SEEDED-VULN CANARY (supply-chain-seeded-test.yml): proves cargo-deny/cargo-audit actually fire on a known-vuln canary — catches the silent-pass mode where the detector works in principle but isn't wired correctly. Gold-standard pattern.
DRIFT DETECTOR SELF-TEST (drift-detect.yml test-drift-detector-fails job): same pattern applied to error-catalog drift.

CROSS-LEG GATE COMPARES ALL PAIRS, NOT ALL TO FIXTURE (ci.yml + determinism.yml): every leg vs every other leg (not just 'each matches the committed fixture') — catches the fixture-drifted-at-commit-time silent-pass.

COLD-INSTALL DELIBERATELY HAS NO RUST (cold-install.yml): a runtime-sanity step asserts cargo is NOT on PATH, so if the runner image ever adds Rust, the test fails loudly rather than silently becoming easier.
CONCURRENCY GROUPS + cancel-in-progress on every workflow: no wasted CI cycles from rapid-push.
--locked enforced in every build step: Cargo.lock drift caught immediately.

test_ignoring_policy.note: 13 Rust tests carry `#[ignore]` with explicit `TODO(phase-2-*)` reason strings naming the concrete Phase-2 dependency blocking them (grant-backed-policy, ivm-replay, hlc, grant-write-API). This is the acceptable pattern — ignored with a reason and a reinstatement plan. HOWEVER: the transform_grammar_fuzz ignore has a DIFFERENT semantics — it's not 'deferred to Phase 2' but 'only runs in a dedicated CI job' — and that job doesn't exist. So 12 of the 13 ignores are healthy; 1 is a silent-pass.

closure_gaps.title: 10k-case TRANSFORM fuzz harness has no CI runner
closure_gaps.fix: Add .github/workflows/fuzz.yml running on nightly cron + workflow_dispatch: `cargo test -p benten-eval --release -- --ignored fuzz_transform_parser` with 10-minute timeout. Without this, R1-security-auditor finding #8 is vacuously satisfied.

closure_gaps.title: No benchmark CI and no perf regression tracking
closure_gaps.fix: Add nightly `bench.yml` running `cargo bench --workspace -- --save-baseline main` on a dedicated runner (or accept a documented noise floor on shared runners) and posting summary as PR comment via criterion-compare-action or bencher.dev. Phase-1 perf targets in plan §Phase-1-Scope are currently unenforced.

closure_gaps.title: PROPTEST_CASES env var designed in but never set in CI
closure_gaps.fix: Set PROPTEST_CASES=10000 in main ci.yml nextest step; add a nightly proptest-heavy job with PROPTEST_CASES=100000 for the landscape-named heavy tests. Currently the 256-default is all that runs.

closure_gaps.title: No mutation testing wired despite skill being installed
closure_gaps.fix: Add mutants.toml to workspace root with surgical exclude-globs (skip test files, benches, the phase-2-stub crates). Add .github/workflows/mutation.yml on weekly cron running `cargo mutants --in-place --baseline skip` on benten-core + benten-eval/src/invariants.rs + benten-eval/src/expr/parser.rs (highest-leverage targets). Target: >80% killed on those files. Phase-1-closure nice-to-have; Phase-2-open hard requirement.

closure_gaps.title: No coverage tooling (cargo-llvm-cov) wired
closure_gaps.fix: Add a nightly `coverage.yml` running `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lcov --output-path lcov.info` and uploading to codecov or keeping as a CI artifact with summary comment. The hand-counted 108/113 coverage claim would be backed by a measured number.

closure_gaps.title: No .config/nextest.toml — CI inherits defaults that can drift
closure_gaps.fix: Add `.config/nextest.toml` pinning `[profile.ci] fail-fast = false, retries = 0, slow-timeout = { period = "60s", terminate-after = 2 }`. The `retries = 0` is load-bearing: the project's flakiness contract is zero hidden flakiness, and nextest retries would mask it.

closure_gaps.title: Document U64_CHAINS global-state contract in CONTRIBUTING.md
closure_gaps.fix: Add a 'Testing' section to CONTRIBUTING.md noting that `cargo-nextest` is LOAD-BEARING for test isolation because it runs each integration binary in its own process. Developers who `cargo test` locally will see different test interaction patterns from CI for the U64_CHAINS-touching tests. Phase-1-closure documentation hygiene.

strengths: Gold-standard seeded-canary pattern on both supply-chain (RUSTSEC-2020-0071 canary) and drift-detector (doctored-fixture self-test) — actively defends against silent-pass failure modes
Cross-leg gate asserts every-vs-every AND every-vs-fixture — both failure modes caught
8-leg (4 arch × 2 Rust) matrix + separate wasm32-wasip1 runtime leg + separate wasm32-unknown-unknown compile-check gives comprehensive target coverage
Cold-install job deliberately has no Rust toolchain and self-asserts that fact — catches the 'we accidentally provisioned a friendly runner' regression
Scaffolder smoke test meta-asserts 6 exit-criterion `it(` blocks in the generated smoke file — regression-safe
All 10 criterion benches use idiomatic `std::hint::black_box` + tuned `measurement_time`/`sample_size` + `iter_batched` where setup needs amortization — good bench hygiene even though no CI runs them
486 Rust #[test]s + 12 proptest! blocks + 6 Vitest files + 102 integration test files is a strong volume for a foundation crate
All 13 #[ignore] tests (except the fuzz one) have explicit TODO(phase-2-*) reason strings naming the concrete reinstatement trigger — excellent discipline
--locked on every build step catches Cargo.lock drift at PR time
concurrency groups + cancel-in-progress on every workflow — no wasted cycles
verdict_rationale: Verdict is CONCERNS, not FAIL. The infrastructure that EXISTS is high-quality and in several places reaches gold-standard patterns (seeded canary, doctored-fixture self-test, every-vs-every determinism gate). Nothing shipped is BROKEN. But three pieces of work are named Phase-1-closure gaps against the R6 test-automation rubric: (1) the 10k fuzz harness not wired to CI is a silent-pass for a named security finding — upgrade to critical; (2) perf claims are unenforced without bench CI; (3) mutation testing is absent despite the skill being present. These are additive, not corrective — no existing test is wrong, but the gaps mean the quality claims in plan §R4 rest partly on discipline rather than CI. A Phase-1-closure PR addressing R6-TA-1 (critical) and R6-TA-3 (PROPTEST_CASES wire-up, major and 30-min effort) would upgrade verdict to PASS.
gate_recommendation: Proceed to Phase 1 close after addressing R6-TA-1 (the fuzz CI gap). R6-TA-2/3/4 can be docketed for a Phase-1.1 hardening sprint or Phase-2 open. R6-TA-5/6/7 are nice-to-have documentation/tooling hygiene, fold into any available cycle.
